criteria.label: State σ(x), its output range, σ(0), and compute σ(x) for given inputs.
Derive and evaluate the sigmoid derivative σ′(x)=σ(x)(1−σ(x)).
Explain saturation and vanishing gradients when sigmoid is stacked in deep hidden layers.
criteria.example: With x=4 in each of 5 sigmoid layers, each σ′≈0.018; product ≈0.018⁵≈2.4×10⁻⁹ — early weights barely update. Fix: ReLU hidden layers.

criteria.label: Choose the correct activation per layer role and implement a stable binary head in PyTorch.
criteria.example: Build nn.Sequential(Linear(d,64), ReLU(), Linear(64,1)); train with BCEWithLogitsLoss(logits, y); at inference return torch.sigmoid(logits) for P(y=1|x) ∈ (0,1).
